_meta.purpose: Client-side reviewer verdicts on past trainer lists (Perplexity-generated or portal-generated). Loaded by pipeline.js at boot; injected as few-shot examples into multiPassRerank when the active brief's keywords overlap a grading's keywords. Goal: bias Sonnet toward the same selectivity standard the client-side reviewer applies, so portal output matches what the client would Select rather than what we think looks good.
_meta.schema: gradings[] = { id, source_brief, reviewer, received_at, keywords[] (for matching against bp.keywords), domain (for matching against bp.domain), verdict_legend{}, candidates[] (each: name, place, linkedin?, headline, verdict in {selected,hold,rejected}, reason — verbatim where possible), rules_distilled[] (3-5 concise reviewer-style rules) }
_meta.how_to_extend: Append new gradings to the array. Each grading is matched independently. Multiple gradings for the same domain are fine — they'll all inject. Keep candidate count under ~12 per grading so the rerank prompt stays compact.

gradings.source_brief: Splunk Observability trainers — Perplexity-generated list sent by Gulshan to client team for review. Not generated by this portal.
gradings.reviewer: Saranya (client-side reviewer at RJP's end client; her team interviews & books the trainers)

verdict_legend.selected: Send to client / book the verification call straight away
verdict_legend.hold: Verification call needed before sending — partial fit only
verdict_legend.rejected: Don't send — known-bad fit (use as negative example)

rules_distilled: SELECT only when BOTH the brief's primary keywords (here: 'Splunk' AND 'Observability') appear in the profile AND the bio explicitly says Trainer (Freelance, Corporate, or Independent).
HOLD for verification call when only one of the two keywords is present, OR role is Consultant rather than explicit Trainer, OR person is currently employed/working IN the tech (not freelance).
REJECT when role is Architect, when profile lacks both required keywords, or when actual location is outside India.
Architect titles, even with deep technical background, do NOT count as bookable trainers in Saranya's framework — they would need explicit additional 'Trainer' or 'Instructor' artefacts.
Geo discipline: 'profile is out of India' overrides the listed Place — Saranya cross-checks LinkedIn directly. Don't trust title-extracted geo alone.
